author: @steves1015
text:
What do you mean by "Ammonia forms as brown streaks"?

Ammonia is colourless.
Stuff like nitrogen dioxide and some nitrates are brown (ish) in colour. Isn't it possible it could be cobalt nitrate being formed instead....

replies.author: @jamesross1003
replies.text: Some of the ammonia produced reacts with the nitrates already present in the solution to produce the brown streaks.  It is a proof positive indicator that ammonia is present.  Not the ammonia itself, just an easy visual way to show that it is in fact being produced.

author: @johnraffensperger
text: This could be very important. Please redo with your narration and implications for scaling up.

replies.author: @canadajim
replies.text: They are building 360 wind turbines near me to power a process like this.

replies.author: @steves1015
replies.text:
This isn't new.
I found a paper from 1998 on this and also a more recent 2015 one.
Trouble is likely to be efficiency and number of side products.
